message:
fix: increase startup probe and pod wait timeouts for IS nightly E2E (#852)

The nightly-e2e-inference-scheduling-ocp workflow (run #37) failed
because 4 of 8 Qwen3-32B decode pods did not become ready within
the 30m pod_wait_timeout. Root cause: 8 pods concurrently downloading
a ~60GB model from HuggingFace into ephemeral emptyDir volumes caused
network contention. One pod on node pokprod-b93r38s1 was killed by
the startup probe (failureThreshold=60 x periodSeconds=30 = 30min)
and restarted, making it impossible to finish within the wait window.

Changes:
- Increase startupProbe failureThreshold from 60 to 120 (30min → 60min)
  to tolerate slow cold-cache model downloads under contention
- Increase pod_wait_timeout from 30m to 60m to match
- Correct required_gpus from 2 to 16 (8 replicas x 2 GPUs each)
  and recommended_gpus from 4 to 16 so the GPU availability check
  reflects actual demand

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -38,9 +38,9 @@ jobs:
       helmfile_env: ${{ github.event.inputs.helmfile_env || 'istio' }}
       gateway_type: ${{ github.event.inputs.helmfile_env || 'istio' }}
       accelerator_type: H100
-      required_gpus: 2
-      recommended_gpus: 4
-      pod_wait_timeout: '30m'
+      required_gpus: 16
+      recommended_gpus: 16
+      pod_wait_timeout: '60m'
       pod_readiness_delay: 180
       image_override: 'ghcr.io/llm-d/llm-d-cuda-dev:latest'
       allow_gpu_preemption: true

@@ -68,7 +68,7 @@ decode:
         initialDelaySeconds: 15
         periodSeconds: 30
         timeoutSeconds: 5
-        failureThreshold: 60
+        failureThreshold: 120
       livenessProbe:
         httpGet:
           path: /health
